beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home
beats.reasoning: Typical Tuesday start; yesterday's energy was satisfied and moderate, so a calm morning at home before the workday. Drizzly weather makes this feel grounded.

beats.activity: Commute to Kirkland dental practice, routing south to avoid SR-520 eastbound closure
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Ingrid leaves early enough to bypass the peak, taking alternate routes (likely I-5 south then 405 north or local roads). Budgets 40 min for a normally 25-min commute due to wet roads and detour caution.

beats.activity: Pediatric dentist appointments and patient care
beats.reasoning: Morning and early afternoon block of scheduled appointments; fits her small-practice rhythm. The rain and low mood (55/100) from yesterday suggest she'll welcome the routine structure and focus on kids' care.

beats.activity: Walk to lunch spot in downtown Kirkland
beats.reasoning: Short 20-min walk/drive from practice to a nearby lunch venue; rain is still steady, so she stays local rather than driving far.

beats.activity: Lunch at a local restaurant, perhaps trying something new
beats.reasoning: Ingrid is an adventurous eater; despite the drizzle, she takes a lunch break at a restaurant she likes. Gives her a mental break before afternoon patients.
Return commute from lunch; rain is tapering by early afternoon per forecast, so a manageable walk/drive back.

beats.activity: Afternoon appointments, admin work, and closing out the practice
beats.reasoning: Afternoon patient block and end-of-day tasks; typical Tuesday rhythm for a small pediatric practice. Gives her time to wind down at work before commuting home.

beats.activity: Commute home from Kirkland to Shoreline
beats.reasoning: Evening commute home; rain has cleared by now (forecast ends at 2pm). Takes standard 40 min, accounting for post-work traffic and wet-road caution.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a simple dinner at home
beats.reasoning: After a full day of work, she's back home for a quiet dinner. Moderate energy (55/100 yesterday) suggests she'll keep it low-key and restorative.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: tend to garden beds and take photos with film camera
beats.reasoning: Weather has cleared; Ingrid values time outdoors with her camera and garden. The rain earlier in the day makes evening light even more dramatic—perfect for photography. This echoes yesterday's satisfying evening rhythm.

diary:
Grey morning, grey day. Woke to that steady drizzle and knew the 520 closure would be a mess, so I routed south through Shoreline—added twenty minutes but beat the worst of it. Coffee helped.

The practice was steady. Had a seven-year-old who was terrified of the chair, but by the end she was laughing. Those moments still matter, even after thirty years. Grabbed lunch at Cafe Juanita, tried their new salmon special. Decent. The afternoon blur of cleanings and fillings, then the usual closing tasks.

Drive home felt long. Rain finally stopped around two but the whole day stayed cold and damp. Made a simple pasta for dinner. After, I spent an hour in the garden beds—the June roses are finally breaking. Took some photos with the old Pentax. There's something about the wet light that film catches differently. Felt peaceful, actually.
thought_process: The 520 closure threw off my usual rhythm, but I'd checked the news early enough to adjust my route without panic. At fifty-nine, I've learned that flexibility saves frustration. Work was routine—pediatric dentistry doesn't offer much surprise—but that scared kid reminded me why I still show up. The rain kept my mood muted but not dark; I actually appreciated the excuse to slow down at lunch. By evening, I needed to do something with my hands that wasn't clinical. The garden and camera work felt like the real medicine today, a way to process the grey and find something intentional in it.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
